traces.prompt:
Write a summary of the results of this experiment based on the information provided below
in a first paragraph starting with "Insights:".

Be concise and stay within 100 words.
Do not mention the name or details of the experiment itself.
Focus only on the improvement or decline of metrics and the reliability of their estimates.

Provide clear, actionable recommendations based on the trends observed
in a second paragraph starting with "Recommendations:".

Leverage your statistical knowledge to analyse each piece of information
before crafting your final output.

If the experiment seems valid — meaning that the setup and data collection worked as intended
(e.g., traffic flowed, metrics were measured, no issues spotted) — then the recommendation can be:
  1. Ship for success if a key metric improves without regressions;
  2. Abort and iterate if regressions are detected and metrics worsen;
  3. Iterate, abandon or ship if it's neutral but informative — when no effect is detected
     but the test was strong enough to detect one if it existed.

Answer in en.
Use only the following results:
Experiment Metadata:

Name: [B2C] [BB] FRENCH10

Variations:
- Original
- NEW

Sample Sizes:
- Original: 2648 sessions
- NEW: 50989 sessions

Unit Type: sessions

Analysis level: Conversion rate

Randomization Method: MAB

Statistical Method Used:
- Inference Approach: Frequentist
- Test Type: Two-tailed
- Confidence Level Required: 95%
- Adjustment for Multiple Comparisons: None


Metric Name: [KAM] [BtoC] revenue

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 0.91%

NEW:
- Value: 0.97%
- Improvement over baseline: 6.68%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 40.78%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 25.1%

 ----------------------------------------

Metric Name: [KAM] [BtoC] revenue - Average Cart

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 1004.38€

NEW:
- Value: 786.24€
- Improvement over baseline: -21.72%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 9.97%
- Statistical Significance: reliability > 99%

  ----------------------------------------

Metric Name: [KAM] [BtoC] revenue - Revenue per Visit

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 9.1€

NEW:
- Value: 7.6€
- Improvement over baseline: -16.49%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 14.49%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 97.43%

 ----------------------------------------



Current Date: 20/06/2026 02:00
traces.response:
Insights:  
The NEW variant showed a slight, non-significant increase in conversion rate (+6.68%, 0.97% vs. 0.91%), but the result is highly uncertain (reliability = 25.1%, CI ±40.78%). However, both average cart value (-21.72%, 786.24€ vs. 1004.38€, reliability >99%) and revenue per visit (-16.49%, 7.6€ vs. 9.1€, reliability = 97.43%) declined significantly, with high statistical confidence.

Recommendations:  
Given the statistically significant declines in both average cart value and revenue per visit, it is recommended to abort this variant and iterate. The observed negative impact on key revenue metrics outweighs the uncertain and non-significant improvement in conversion rate.

Answer in en.
Use only the following results:
Experiment Metadata:

Name: APHI Q&E Checkout Page: Changing "Complete Enrollment" Button Copy A/B Test

Variations:
- Original
- "Purchase"
- "Enroll"
- "Checkout"

Sample Sizes:
- Original: 18172 users
- "Purchase": 18194 users
- "Enroll": 18084 users
- "Checkout": 18136 users

Unit Type: users

Analysis level: Conversion rate

Randomization Method: MAB

Statistical Method Used:
- Inference Approach: Frequentist
- Test Type: Two-tailed
- Confidence Level Required: 95%
- Adjustment for Multiple Comparisons: None


Metric Name: GWP (APHI)

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 3.33%

"Purchase":
- Value: 3.41%
- Improvement over baseline: 2.19%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 11.01%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 29.99%

"Enroll":
- Value: 3.43%
- Improvement over baseline: 2.97%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 11.0%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 39.84%

"Checkout":
- Value: 3.35%
- Improvement over baseline: 0.53%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 11.06%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 7.45%

 ----------------------------------------

Metric Name: GWP (APHI) - Average Cart

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 115.63$

"Purchase":
- Value: 108.0$
- Improvement over baseline: -6.6%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 11.63%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 73.35%

"Enroll":
- Value: 112.32$
- Improvement over baseline: -2.86%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 12.97%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 33.5%

"Checkout":
- Value: 140.2$
- Improvement over baseline: 21.25%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 22.52%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 93.55%

  ----------------------------------------

Metric Name: GWP (APHI) - Revenue per Visit

- CUPED: OFF

Baseline (Original):
- Value: 3.86$

"Purchase":
- Value: 3.68$
- Improvement over baseline: -4.55%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 14.02%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 47.56%

"Enroll":
- Value: 3.86$
- Improvement over baseline: 0.02%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 15.45%
- Statistical Significance: reliability < 1%

"Checkout":
- Value: 4.7$
- Improvement over baseline: 21.89%
- Confidence Interval: 95% CI: +/- 24.54%
- Statistical Significance: reliability = 91.96%

 ----------------------------------------



Current Date: 20/06/2026 02:00
traces.response:
Insights:  
None of the tested variants showed statistically significant improvements in conversion rate over the baseline, with all reliabilities well below the 95% threshold ("Purchase": 29.99%, "Enroll": 39.84%, "Checkout": 7.45%). For average cart value, only the "Checkout" variant showed a notable positive lift (+21.25%) with high reliability (93.55%), but this did not reach the required significance level. Revenue per visit also favored "Checkout" (+21.89%, reliability = 91.96%), yet this result remains just below the confidence threshold.

Recommendations:  
Given the lack of statistically significant improvements in key metrics, no variant can be confidently recommended for rollout at this stage. The "Checkout" variant shows promising trends in cart value and revenue per visit, but further testing with larger samples or refined hypotheses is advised before considering implementation. Continue monitoring and iterating based on these preliminary signals.

traces.prompt:
Write a summary of the results of this experiment based on the information provided below
in a first paragraph starting with "Insights:".

Be concise and stay within 100 words.
Do not mention the name or details of the experiment itself.
Focus only on the improvement or decline of metrics and the reliability of their estimates.

Provide clear, actionable recommendations based on the trends observed
in a second paragraph starting with "Recommendations:".

Leverage your statistical knowledge to analyse each piece of information
before crafting your final output.

If the experiment seems valid — meaning that the setup and data collection worked as intended
(e.g., traffic flowed, metrics were measured, no issues spotted) — then the recommendation can be:
  1. Ship for success if a key metric improves without regressions;
  2. Abort and iterate if regressions are detected and metrics worsen;
  3. Iterate, abandon or ship if it's neutral but informative — when no effect is detected
     but the test was strong enough to detect one if it existed.

traces.response:
Insights:  
Across all measured metrics, differences between the Original and Variation 1 were small and not statistically significant, with reliabilities well below the 95% threshold. The primary conversion rate (Purchase) showed a +4.81% lift (9.92% vs. 9.46%), but with extremely low reliability (<1%). Revenue per visit and average cart value both declined slightly in Variation 1, also without statistical significance. The largest observed lift was in "Brand Search Open" (+41.0%), but this result approached, yet did not reach, statistical significance (reliability = 92.57%). All other metrics showed minimal or inconsistent changes with wide confidence intervals.

Recommendations:  
No actionable effect was detected for Variation 1, as none of the observed changes reached statistical significance. It is recommended to iterate or abandon this variation, as the test was sufficiently powered to detect meaningful differences but found none. Further testing or alternative approaches may be needed to achieve measurable impact.

traces.prompt:
Is it possible to go back to previous settings in a personnalization after we changed them?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 17:47
traces.response:
Based on the Kameleoon documentation, there is no direct feature to "go back" or "undo" changes to previous settings within a personalization after you have changed and saved them. However, there are a couple of related options:

1. **Reset Project Default Options:** In the project management settings, you can reset the project's default options, but this does not revert individual personalizations to previous states—it only resets the default settings for new personalizations. See: [Manage your projects - Personalization](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/project-management/manage-your-projects#personalization).

2. **Restore Archived Personalizations:** If you have archived a personalization, you can restore it from the archived view. This allows you to bring back a previous version if you had archived it before making changes. See: [Manage personalizations](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/web-personalization/manage-personalizations).

There is no mention in the documentation of a version history or an "undo" feature for reverting changes made to an active personalization. If you need to revert to a previous configuration, you would need to manually reapply the previous settings or restore an archived version if available.

If you need more advanced version control, consider duplicating your personalization before making significant changes, so you can always go back to the previous version if needed.

traces.prompt:
What personalization options are available through your tool?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 16:21
traces.response:
Kameleoon offers a wide range of personalization options to tailor experiences for your website visitors. Here are the main personalization features available:

1. **Personalization Campaigns**: You can create and manage personalized experiences for different visitor segments. These can include content changes, pop-ins, banners, or other dynamic elements.

2. **Targeting & Segmentation**: Personalizations can be targeted based on a variety of criteria, such as behavior, demographics, device, location, and custom data.

3. **Consent Management**: You can define consent policies for personalizations, including behavior when consent is unknown or when a user opts out. This ensures compliance with privacy regulations.
   - More details: /user-manual/project-management/consent-management-policy

4. **Control Groups**: Exclude a percentage of visitors from personalizations to measure their effectiveness against a control group.
   - More details: /user-manual/project-management/manage-your-projects#personalization

5. **Advanced Display Options**: Control whether multiple personalizations can be shown simultaneously on the same page, or restrict to only one at a time.

6. **Priority by Personalization**: For product recommendations, you can prioritize products that match each visitor’s preferences using industry-specific tags and behavioral data.
   - More details: /user-manual/recommendations-and-merchandising/recommendations/strategy-settings#priority-by-personalization

7. **General Settings**: Configure simulation URLs, add tags and descriptions, use custom scripts, and set attribution windows for each personalization.
   - More details: /user-manual/web-personalization/set-up-and-launch-a-personalization#step-4-configure-general-settings-optional

traces.prompt:
может ли быть одинаковый siteCode для абсолютно разных сайтов

Current Date: 19/06/2026 16:09
traces.response:
Да, **siteCode** в Kameleoon может быть один и тот же для абсолютно разных сайтов, если вы этого хотите. Это называется использованием одного проекта (siteCode) для нескольких доменов.

### Как это работает

- **siteCode** — это уникальный идентификатор проекта в Kameleoon.
- Один и тот же siteCode можно вставить в код на разных сайтах/доменах.
- Все данные (эксперименты, таргетинги, сегменты, цели, аудитории и т.д.) будут общими для всех сайтов, где используется этот siteCode.
- В интерфейсе Kameleoon вы будете видеть статистику и управлять экспериментами для всех сайтов сразу.

### Когда это удобно

- Если у вас несколько сайтов с похожей аудиторией и вы хотите проводить эксперименты или персонализации сразу на всех.
- Если вы хотите централизованно управлять экспериментами и сегментами для группы сайтов.

### Когда это не рекомендуется

- Если сайты абсолютно разные по тематике, аудитории, целям или владельцам.
- Если нужно разделять аналитику, цели, эксперименты и доступы между сайтами.
- Если есть риск случайно запустить эксперимент не на том сайте.

### Официальная документация и рекомендации

- Kameleoon поддерживает оба подхода: один siteCode на несколько доменов или отдельный siteCode для каждого сайта.
- Подробнее о плюсах и минусах:  
  [Single Kameleoon project across domains vs. multiple projects](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/web-experimentation/best-practices/single-kameleoon-project-across-domains-versus-multiple-projects)

---

**Вывод:**  
Вы можете использовать один и тот же siteCode для разных сайтов, но делайте это только если вам действительно нужно объединить аналитику и управление экспериментами для этих сайтов. В противном случае лучше использовать отдельный siteCode для каждого сайта.

traces.prompt:
как можно передать visitorCode между доменами

Current Date: 19/06/2026 15:49
traces.response:
Передача visitorCode между доменами (разными siteCode) в Kameleoon возможна только вручную — стандартная client-side интеграция не синхронизирует идентификаторы между проектами автоматически. Вот основные способы, которые рекомендует Kameleoon:

---

## 1. Через URL-параметр

- При редиректе с одного домена на другой добавьте visitorCode в URL:
  ```
  https://new-domain.com/?visitorCode=abc123
  ```
- На новом домене при инициализации Kameleoon SDK или скрипта получите этот параметр из URL и установите его как visitorCode:
  ```js
  // Получить visitorCode из URL
  const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const visitorCode = urlParams.get('visitorCode');
  if (visitorCode) {
    window.kameleoonQueue = window.kameleoonQueue || [];
    window.kameleoonQueue.push({
      level: "IMMEDIATE",
      command: () => Kameleoon.API.Visitor.setVisitorCode(visitorCode)
    });
  }
  ```
  [Документация: setVisitorCode](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/apis/activation-api-js/api-reference/api-reference#setvisitorcode)

---

## 2. Через backend (рекомендуется для авторизованных пользователей)

- Сохраняйте visitorCode пользователя на сервере (например, в базе или сессии).
- При переходе пользователя на другой домен, отдавайте visitorCode через backend (например, через cookie, API или серверный рендеринг).
- На новом домене используйте этот visitorCode при инициализации Kameleoon SDK (см. пример выше).

---

## 3. Через cookie (если домены имеют общий родительский домен)

- Если оба домена являются поддоменами одного основного домена (например, a.example.com и b.example.com), можно использовать cookie с domain=.example.com.
- Для разных доменов (example1.com и example2.com) этот способ не работает из-за ограничений браузеров.

---

## 4. Через Single Sign-On (SSO) или централизованную авторизацию

- Если у пользователя есть глобальный userID (например, через SSO), используйте его как visitorCode на всех доменах.
- Это обеспечивает кросс-доменную идентификацию пользователя.

---

## Важно:
- Передавайте visitorCode как можно раньше, до инициализации экспериментов на новом домене.
- visitorCode должен быть уникальным для каждого пользователя и не превышать 255 символов.

---

### Документация и примеры:
- [Code examples: Pass visitor code between layers](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/get-started/cross-platform-sdks#code-examples-pass-visitor-code-between-layers)
- [setVisitorCode](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/apis/activation-api-js/api-reference/api-reference#setvisitorcode)

---

Если нужен конкретный пример для вашей платформы (JS, Node.js, PHP, mobile SDK) — уточните, и я предоставлю код.

traces.prompt:
как связан visitorCode пользователя и siteCode

Current Date: 19/06/2026 15:47
traces.response:
Связь между visitorCode и siteCode в Kameleoon следующая:

- **siteCode** — это уникальный идентификатор проекта в Kameleoon. Каждый проект (например, отдельный сайт или приложение) имеет свой siteCode.
- **visitorCode** — это уникальный идентификатор посетителя внутри конкретного проекта (siteCode). Он используется для отслеживания действий пользователя, назначения вариаций экспериментов и сбора статистики.

### Как это работает:

- При инициализации SDK или загрузке Kameleoon-скрипта вы всегда указываете siteCode, чтобы SDK/скрипт знал, к какому проекту относится текущий сайт.
- visitorCode может быть:
  - Сгенерирован автоматически Kameleoon (по умолчанию, если пользователь анонимен).
  - Передан явно (например, если у вас есть свой userID — рекомендуется для авторизованных пользователей).
- visitorCode уникален только в рамках одного siteCode (проекта). Один и тот же пользователь на двух разных проектах (siteCode) получит два разных visitorCode, если вы не реализуете передачу visitorCode вручную.

### Пример инициализации SDK:
```js
const siteCode = "ваш_siteCode";
const visitorCode = "user123"; // ваш уникальный ID пользователя, если есть

const kameleoonClient = KameleoonClientFactory.create(siteCode, visitorCode, config);
```
Подробнее:  
- [Code examples: Pass visitor code between layers](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/get-started/cross-platform-sdks#code-examples-pass-visitor-code-between-layers)
- [Документация по инициализации SDK](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/sdks/mobile-sdks/android-sdk#java-10)

### Важно:
- Если вы хотите отслеживать одного пользователя между разными проектами (siteCode), вам нужно самостоятельно синхронизировать visitorCode между этими проектами (например, через backend или параметры URL).
- Без такой синхронизации один и тот же пользователь будет считаться разными посетителями в разных проектах.

---

**Вывод:**  
visitorCode всегда "привязан" к конкретному siteCode. Для каждого проекта (siteCode) visitorCode уникален и не связан автоматически с visitorCode в другом проекте.

traces.prompt:
как настроить редирект эксперимент с одного домена на другой? У проектов разный sitecoode

Current Date: 19/06/2026 15:36
traces.response:
В официальной документации Kameleoon нет прямой инструкции по настройке редирект-эксперимента между двумя доменами, у которых разные sitecode (разные проекты). Однако, есть важные рекомендации и ограничения, которые нужно учитывать:

### Основные моменты и best practices

1. **Редирект между доменами с разными sitecode**:
   - Kameleoon не поддерживает "сквозные" эксперименты между проектами с разными sitecode. Каждый проект (sitecode) работает независимо, и посетитель, попавший на новый домен, будет рассматриваться как новый пользователь для второго проекта.
   - Если вы делаете редирект с одного домена на другой, то после перехода на новый домен Kameleoon не сможет автоматически "связать" визитора между двумя проектами. Это важно для корректного сбора статистики и атрибуции конверсий.

2. **SEO и технические рекомендации**:
   - Не делайте редирект 100% трафика на новый URL.
   - Используйте только штатные средства редиректа Kameleoon (`Kameleoon.API.Core.processRedirect("newURL")`), а не `document.location.href`.
   - На обеих страницах указывайте канонический URL.
   - Не используйте тег `noindex` на страницах эксперимента.
   - Подробнее: [FAQ: Redirection guidelines](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/faq#redirection-guidelines) и [URL redirection and SEO impact](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/url-redirection-split-test#url-redirection-and-seo-impact).

3. **Передача visitorCode между проектами**:
   - Если вам критично отслеживать одного пользователя между двумя доменами/проектами, потребуется реализовать передачу visitorCode через backend или параметры URL, и вручную инициализировать Kameleoon на втором домене с этим visitorCode. Пример для SDK: [Code examples: Pass visitor code between layers](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/get-started/cross-platform-sdks#code-examples-pass-visitor-code-between-layers).
   - Это требует серверной логики и не поддерживается "из коробки" для client-side экспериментов.

4. **Лучшее решение**:
   - Если у вас часто возникают задачи с редиректом между доменами, рассмотрите возможность объединения доменов в один проект (sitecode), если это допустимо с точки зрения архитектуры и политики компании. Подробнее: [Single Kameleoon project across domains vs. multiple projects](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/web-experimentation/best-practices/single-kameleoon-project-across-domains-versus-multiple-projects).

---

### Краткая инструкция (если всё же нужно сделать редирект между двумя проектами):

1. В проекте-источнике (sitecode1) создайте эксперимент с редиректом:
   ```js
   Kameleoon.API.Core.processRedirect("https://target-domain.com/landing-page");
   ```
2. На целевом домене (sitecode2) настройте отдельный эксперимент или отслеживание, но статистика будет раздельной.
3. Для передачи visitorCode потребуется реализовать серверную синхронизацию (см. пример выше).

---

**Вывод:**  
Kameleoon не поддерживает "сквозные" редирект-эксперименты между проектами с разными sitecode на уровне client-side без дополнительной серверной логики. Для корректной работы и сбора данных используйте рекомендации из разделов FAQ и best practices:

- [Redirection guidelines](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/faq#redirection-guidelines)
- [Single Kameleoon project across domains vs. multiple projects](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/web-experimentation/best-practices/single-kameleoon-project-across-domains-versus-multiple-projects)
- [Code examples: Pass visitor code between layers](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/get-started/cross-platform-sdks#code-examples-pass-visitor-code-between-layers)

Если нужна конкретная схема передачи visitorCode или интеграции между двумя проектами — уточните, и я помогу с примером кода.

traces.prompt:
why cant i set a test live

Current Date: 19/06/2026 15:17
traces.response:
There are several reasons why you might not be able to set a test (experiment) live in Kameleoon. Here are the most common causes and troubleshooting steps:

### 1. Script Not Installed or Not Updated
- **Kameleoon script must be installed and up-to-date** on your website for experiments to run live. If the script is missing or cached, your changes may not appear.
- Clear your browser cache and verify the script is present and current.

### 2. Configuration or Targeting Issues
- **Targeting criteria may not be met**: If your experiment targets a specific segment and your session does not match, you won’t see the experiment live.
- **Reference variation**: You might be assigned to the control group (reference variation), so you won’t see changes.
- Use the simulation panel or force a variation for testing.

### 3. Consent and Legal Requirements
- If your project requires legal consent (GDPR, etc.), you must call the `setLegalConsent(true)` method in your SDK or implementation. Without consent, experiments may not run or collect data.

### 4. Scheduling and Status
- If you scheduled your experiment for a future date/time, it will not go live until that time.
- If the experiment is paused or ended, it cannot be set live until resumed.

### 5. Credit or Plan Limitations (PBX)
- If you are using Prompt-Based Experimentation (PBX) and have no credits left, you cannot create or launch new experiments.

### 6. Content Security Policy (CSP) or Browser Issues
- Strict CSP settings or browser extensions (like ad blockers) may block Kameleoon scripts or cookies, preventing experiments from running live.

### 7. Technical Errors or Delays
- There may be a delay (up to 10 minutes) for experiments to go live after launch.
- Check for any error messages in the Kameleoon interface or browser console.

---

**Recommended Troubleshooting Steps:**
- Ensure the Kameleoon script is installed and up-to-date.
- Check your experiment’s targeting and configuration.
- Make sure you have provided legal consent if required.
- Verify your experiment is not scheduled for a future date or paused.
- Check for any credit limitations if using PBX.
- Review your browser’s CSP and disable ad blockers for testing.
- Wait a few minutes after launching and clear your cache.

For more details, see:
- [I launched an experiment but cannot see changes on my website](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/faq#i-launched-an-experiment-but-cannot-see-changes-on-my-website)
- [Consent management policy](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/project-management/consent-management-policy)
- [Credit-based limitations for PBX](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/prompt-based-experiments/pbx-build/prompt-based-experimentation-plans#credit-based-limitations)

If you are still unable to set your test live, please provide any error messages or describe your setup for more targeted help.

traces.prompt:
what mcp tools can you use?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 14:51
traces.response:
Here are the main MCP (Management Control Panel) tools I can use for managing and automating Kameleoon platform entities:

1. Experiments (A/B Tests)
   - Create, list, and get details of experiments.
   - Attach targeting rules (segments/triggers).
   - Retrieve experiment code and results.
   - Note: Directly adding/removing goals to an existing experiment is not supported via API.

2. Feature Flags (Full Stack)
   - Create, list, duplicate, enable/disable, and delete feature flags.
   - Manage variations and variables.
   - Add delivery rules (targeted, progressive, experimentation).
   - Retrieve activity logs and experiment results for flags.

3. Goals
   - Create, list, search, get, update, and delete goals.
   - Change goal type and configuration.
   - Note: Can’t directly attach/detach goals to experiments via API.

4. Segments
   - Create, list, get, and delete segments.
   - Manage segment condition trees.

5. Targeting Rules
   - Create, list, and get targeting rules for experiments.

6. Sites (Projects)
   - List all accessible sites (projects) and retrieve their codes.

7. Personalizations, Holdouts, and User Data
   - Retrieve metadata and some configuration (via GraphQL tool).

8. Documentation Search
   - Search and read Kameleoon documentation for implementation, SDKs, APIs, and best practices.

If you want to perform a specific action, let me know what you’d like to do, and I’ll let you know if it’s possible with the available tools or guide you through the process!

For more details, see:
- https://developers.kameleoon.com/
- https://help.kameleoon.com/experiment-management/
- https://help.kameleoon.com/feature-flags/

traces.prompt:
why does my test have triggered in visit positive but say there is not a triggered ?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 14:47
traces.response:
If your test shows "triggered in visit" as positive but does not show as "triggered," it means the experiment was activated (triggered) during the current session/visit, but it is not considered as triggered for the visitor overall (across all visits).

This can happen due to several reasons:

1. **Session Scope vs. Visitor Scope**: "Triggered in visit" only checks if the experiment was triggered in the current session. "Triggered" checks if the experiment has ever been triggered for the visitor, possibly across multiple sessions.

2. **Capping or Targeting Conditions**: If your experiment uses capping (limits on how often it can be shown) or specific targeting conditions, it may only trigger in certain visits. For example, if a visitor only qualifies for the experiment in the current session, it will show as "triggered in visit" but not as "triggered" for the visitor in general.

3. **Data Synchronization or Timing**: There may be a delay or difference in how the two statuses are updated, especially if you are checking them at different times or with different API calls.

4. **Experiment Reset or Data Removal**: If visitor data was reset or removed between visits, the "triggered" status may not persist, but "triggered in visit" will reflect the current session's state.

For more details on how these statuses are determined, see:
- https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/apis/activation-api-js/api-reference/api-reference#gettriggeredinvisit

If you need to troubleshoot further, check your experiment's capping, targeting, and data retention settings.

traces.prompt:
what is the difference between a test being triggered versus triggered in visit?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 14:46
traces.response:
The difference between a test being "triggered" and "triggered in visit" in Kameleoon is as follows:

- **Triggered**: This generally refers to an experiment (test) being activated for a visitor at any point—meaning the visitor has met the targeting conditions and the experiment has run for them, regardless of when or how many times.

- **Triggered in Visit**: This is more specific and refers to experiments that have been triggered during the current visit (session) of the user. For example, the method Kameleoon.API.Experiments.getTriggeredInVisit() returns all experiments that were triggered during the current visit/session only.

So, "triggered" could refer to any time in the visitor's history, while "triggered in visit" is limited to the current session.

Reference: 
- https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/apis/activation-api-js/api-reference/api-reference#gettriggeredinvisit

traces.prompt:
quelle est la différence entre un segment et un trigger

Current Date: 19/06/2026 14:27
traces.response:
Voici la différence entre un segment et un trigger dans Kameleoon :

- Un segment est un groupe de visiteurs défini par un ensemble de conditions (ex : visiteurs venant de France, utilisant un mobile, ayant visité une page spécifique, etc.). Les segments servent à cibler une audience précise pour vos expériences ou personnalisations. Les conditions d’un segment sont généralement basées sur des attributs ou comportements du visiteur, et la segmentation s’effectue avant l’activation de l’expérience. Pour plus de détails : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/segments/create-a-segment

- Un trigger (déclencheur) est une condition ou un événement qui va activer l’expérience ou la personnalisation pour un visiteur qui appartient déjà au segment ciblé. Par exemple, le trigger peut être le fait de cliquer sur un bouton, d’atteindre une page précise, ou de remplir un formulaire. Le trigger intervient donc après la segmentation, pour déterminer le moment précis où l’expérience doit se déclencher. Pour plus d’informations : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/triggers/set-up-a-trigger-in-a-campaign

En résumé :
- Segment = Qui ? (audience ciblée)
- Trigger = Quand ? (événement ou condition d’activation)

Pour voir comment Kameleoon distingue et combine ces deux notions dans la configuration d’une expérience, consultez : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/prompt-based-experiments/pbx-build/pbx-configure#combine-segment-and-trigger-instructions-in-one-prompt

traces.prompt:
Peux-tu m'aider à créer une variation sur une page afin de descendre d 'un niveau un bloc pour en mettre un au dessus ?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 12:58
traces.response:
Pour créer une variation sur une page Kameleoon afin de descendre un bloc et d’en mettre un autre au-dessus, voici les étapes à suivre selon la documentation officielle :

### 1. Ouvrir l’éditeur graphique Kameleoon
- Lancez l’éditeur graphique sur la page concernée.
- Sélectionnez la variation sur laquelle vous souhaitez travailler (par exemple, “Variation 1”).

### 2. Sélectionner et déplacer le bloc
- Cliquez sur le bloc que vous souhaitez déplacer.
- Utilisez l’option de positionnement pour choisir où placer ce bloc par rapport aux autres éléments de la page.
- Vous pouvez choisir de placer un élément “Avant”, “Après” ou “Remplacer” un autre élément de référence.
- Pour insérer un nouveau bloc au-dessus, sélectionnez l’élément de référence (celui qui doit rester en dessous) et choisissez “Avant” pour placer le nouveau bloc au-dessus.

> “Vous pouvez choisir la nouvelle position de l’élément par rapport à d’autres éléments. Les options incluent ‘À l’intérieur de la page’ ou ‘Au-dessus de la page’, et peuvent être définies comme ‘Relative’ ou ‘Absolue’. Spécifiez la position exacte par rapport à un élément de référence sélectionné (par exemple, avant, remplacer ou après).”  
— Documentation : /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/graphic-based-experiments/edit-a-variation#select-position-of-the-element

### 3. Ajouter un nouveau bloc (si besoin)
- Utilisez l’option “Ajouter un élément” dans l’éditeur graphique pour insérer un nouveau bloc (texte, image, bouton, etc.).
- Placez ce nouveau bloc à l’endroit souhaité en utilisant la même logique de positionnement.

### 4. Sauvegarder et tester la variation
- Enregistrez vos modifications.
- Utilisez la fonction de prévisualisation pour vérifier que le nouvel ordre des blocs correspond à vos attentes.

### Documentation utile :
- Gestion des variations : /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/graphic-based-experiments/add-and-manage-variations
- Positionnement des éléments : /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/graphic-based-experiments/edit-a-variation#select-position-of-the-element

Si vous souhaitez faire ce changement via du code (JS/CSS), faites-le dans l’éditeur de code de la variation. Par exemple, pour déplacer un bloc via JavaScript, vous pouvez utiliser :

```javascript
Kameleoon.API.Core.runWhenElementPresent("#bloc-a-deplacer, #bloc-reference", function(elements) {
  var blocADescendre = document.querySelector("#bloc-a-deplacer");
  var blocReference = document.querySelector("#bloc-reference");
  blocReference.parentNode.insertBefore(blocADescendre, blocReference);
});
```

Pour plus de détails sur l’utilisation du code dans une variation :  
/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/code-based-experiments/using-the-code-editor

N’hésitez pas à préciser si vous souhaitez une méthode graphique ou par code, ou si vous avez des sélecteurs spécifiques à manipuler !

traces.prompt:
I'm setting up an experiment to test a new carousel component for our mobile navigation only. Is there a way to set up a click based goal for the new component? I struggle to set up goals specifically for mobile only components (as our navigation is completely different) in general and especially for this new component.

Current Date: 19/06/2026 12:56
traces.response:
Yes, you can set up a click-based goal for your new carousel component that is specific to mobile navigation in Kameleoon. Here’s how to approach this, including tips for targeting mobile-only components:

### 1. Setting Up a Click Goal for the Carousel

- Go to the Goals section in Kameleoon and create a new goal.
- Choose the **Click tracking** goal type.
- Enter the URL where your mobile carousel appears.
- Select the carousel element directly or enter its unique CSS selector. If your mobile navigation uses a different DOM structure, make sure the selector is unique to the mobile version (e.g., `.mobile-carousel` or a specific ID).
- If the element only exists on mobile, you may need to use device emulation in your browser or resize your window to mobile dimensions to select it.

> Kameleoon tracks clicks on the selected element across your entire website. To track clicks on an element on a specific page or context (like mobile), use a CSS selector unique to that context.  
> [Read more about click tracking goals](/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#click-tracking)

### 2. Targeting Mobile-Only Users

To ensure your experiment and goal only apply to mobile users:

- In the experiment’s targeting settings, add a condition to target only mobile devices.
- You can do this by:
  - Using the **Device** targeting condition and selecting "Smartphone" (and/or "Tablet" if needed).
  - Or, if you use segments, create a segment for mobile users and target that segment.

> You can build a segment with AI by describing your audience as "Mobile users," or use the device targeting condition directly.  
> [See how to configure targeting](/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/prompt-based-experiments/pbx-build/pbx-configure)

### 3. Tips for Mobile-Only Component Goals

- Make sure the CSS selector you use for click tracking is unique to the mobile navigation. If your desktop and mobile navigations share selectors, you might need to add a custom attribute or class to the mobile carousel for precise tracking.
- If your mobile navigation is rendered conditionally (e.g., only below a certain viewport width), use browser dev tools to simulate a mobile device when setting up the goal.

### 4. Applying the Goal to Your Experiment

- Once your goal is created, apply it to your experiment in the finalization step.
- Select your click goal from the list and proceed.

> [How to set up a goal in an experiment](/user-manual/assets/goals/set-up-a-goal-campaign)

---

**Summary:**  
- Use a unique CSS selector for your mobile carousel when creating a click goal.
- Target only mobile users in your experiment settings.
- Apply the goal to your experiment.

If you need more advanced targeting (e.g., only when a specific mobile element is present), you can create a segment based on the presence of that element on the page.

**References:**  
- [Click tracking goal setup](/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#click-tracking)  
- [Configure targeting for mobile users](/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/prompt-based-experiments/pbx-build/pbx-configure)  
- [Set up a goal in an experiment](/user-manual/assets/goals/set-up-a-goal-campaign)

Let me know if you need help with CSS selectors or segment creation!

traces.prompt:
dans l'éditeur graphique, il est possible de faire un test  dans plusieurs pages en changeant de page: donne moi des exemples pertinents de ce type de test

Current Date: 19/06/2026 12:26
traces.response:
Voici des exemples pertinents de tests que vous pouvez réaliser sur plusieurs pages en utilisant l’éditeur graphique de Kameleoon, en changeant de page pendant la création du test :

1. **Uniformisation d’un élément sur plusieurs pages**  
   Exemple : Modifier le texte ou la couleur d’un bouton « Ajouter au panier » sur toutes les pages produits d’un site e-commerce. Vous pouvez naviguer d’une page produit à l’autre dans l’éditeur graphique et appliquer la même modification à chaque page.

2. **Test de bannière ou message global**  
   Exemple : Ajouter une bannière promotionnelle ou un message d’alerte (ex : livraison offerte) sur toutes les pages du tunnel de conversion (panier, livraison, paiement). Vous pouvez ainsi vérifier l’impact de ce message sur le taux de conversion global.

3. **Optimisation du menu ou du header sur tout le site**  
   Exemple : Modifier la structure du menu principal ou la couleur du header sur toutes les pages principales (accueil, catégories, produits, etc.) pour tester l’impact sur la navigation et l’engagement.

4. **Modification d’un formulaire multi-étapes**  
   Exemple : Sur un formulaire d’inscription ou de commande en plusieurs étapes (pages différentes), tester une simplification ou un changement de wording sur chaque étape pour voir l’impact sur le taux de complétion.

5. **Test sur des pages partageant la même structure mais des URLs différentes**  
   Exemple : Modifier un bloc de recommandations sur toutes les pages de catégorie ou de blog, même si les URLs sont différentes, en appliquant la modification sur chaque page via l’éditeur graphique.

Pour cibler plusieurs pages, vous pouvez utiliser les conditions de ciblage avancées dans Kameleoon, comme « l’URL contient », « correspond exactement à », ou « correspond à une expression régulière ». Cela permet d’appliquer le test à un ensemble de pages partageant une structure ou un motif d’URL commun.

Pour plus de détails et d’exemples sur la configuration de tests multi-pages, consultez la documentation officielle :  
- [Multi-page experiments : Several pages sharing the same URL](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/multi-page-experiments)

N’hésitez pas à préciser votre cas d’usage si vous souhaitez des exemples adaptés à votre secteur ou à votre site !

Peux-tu me sortir la documentation pour créer des objectifs via GTM ?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 12:25
traces.response:
Voici la documentation officielle pour créer des objectifs (goals) Kameleoon via Google Tag Manager (GTM) :

### Documentation principale
- **Créer une conversion d’objectif depuis GTM**  
  Cette page explique comment créer des tags de conversion Kameleoon dans Google Tag Manager pour suivre des événements, du chiffre d’affaires et des métadonnées, sans modifier directement le code de votre site.  
  👉 Documentation : [/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal-conversion-tag-from-gtm](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal-conversion-tag-from-gtm)

### Exemple de code à utiliser dans GTM
Dans votre Tag Manager, créez un déclencheur sur la page de confirmation (ou l’événement souhaité) et ajoutez le code suivant dans une balise HTML personnalisée :

```javascript
// Remplacez kameleoonGoalID et {{revenue}} par vos valeurs réelles
window.kameleoonQueue = window.kameleoonQueue || [];
window.kameleoonQueue.push(['Goals.processConversion', kameleoonGoalID, {{revenue}}])
```
Source : [/developer-docs/web-experimentation/best-practices/best-practices-for-ab-testing#examples](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/web-experimentation/best-practices/best-practices-for-ab-testing#examples)

### Points importants
- Vous pouvez suivre différents types d’objectifs : clic, scroll, accès à une page, objectif personnalisé, temps passé, nombre de pages vues, taux de rétention, etc.
- Pour chaque objectif, il suffit de pousser l’événement correspondant dans la queue Kameleoon via une balise GTM.
- Si vous souhaitez envoyer l’événement à Google Analytics 4 via GTM, il faudra configurer la balise GA4 Event dans GTM pour relayer l’événement.

N’hésitez pas à consulter la documentation détaillée pour chaque étape et à adapter le code à vos besoins spécifiques !

Current Date: 19/06/2026 11:54
traces.response:
Based on your description, you are only getting 5 archived tests back when running your function to pull all archived experiments. Here are the most likely causes and solutions, based on Kameleoon documentation and your code:

---

### 1. **Pagination Parameters**
- The Kameleoon API uses pagination for listing experiments and feature flags.
- By default, the API may return only a limited number of items per request (often 20 or 100).
- You are using `size=100` and incrementing `from` by 100, which is correct for offset-based pagination.
- However, the documentation mentions that the default page size is 20 and the maximum is 200 for some endpoints. You may want to try `size=200` to maximize results per call.

**Reference:**  
[Pagination](/developer-docs/apis/automation-api-rest/get-started/get-started#pagination)

---

### 2. **Filtering for Archived Experiments**
- Your code filters for experiments where `status` is `"stopped"` or `"paused"` or `isArchived === true`.
- However, not all stopped or paused experiments are archived. You may want to explicitly filter for archived experiments if the API supports it.
- The API may also require a specific filter parameter to return only archived experiments.

---

### 3. **API Endpoint and Version**
- Ensure you are using the correct endpoint and version. For experiments, you use `/experiments?v=2.0&size=100&from=...`.
- For feature flags, you use `/feature-flags?size=100&from=...`.
- If the API has changed or if your project has more than 1000 archived items, you may need to adjust your logic.

---

### 4. **Site ID Filtering**
- If you provide a `siteId`, ensure it is correct and that you have archived experiments for that site.

---

### 5. **Check for More Results**
- If you have more than 1000 archived experiments, your code will stop after 10 pages (`if (expFrom >= 1000) keepGoingExps = false;`). Remove or increase this limit if needed.

---

### 6. **API Response Structure**
- Double-check the structure of the API response. Sometimes, the data may be nested under a different property (e.g., `experiments`, `data`, etc.).

---

### 7. **Manual Verification**
- Try calling the API manually (e.g., with Postman or curl) to see how many archived experiments are returned for your site.

---

## **Suggested Fixes**

- Increase `size` to 200 in your API calls.
- Remove or increase the `expFrom >= 1000` and `featFrom >= 1000` limits if you expect more results.
- Double-check your filtering logic for archived status.
- Review the API documentation for any new or changed parameters for filtering archived experiments.

---

## **Example: Adjusted Pagination**

```javascript
const pageSize = 200; // Use the maximum allowed by the API
let expFrom = 0;
let keepGoingExps = true;

while (keepGoingExps) {
  let url = `https://api.kameleoon.com/experiments?v=2.0&size=${pageSize}&from=${expFrom}`;
  // ... rest of your code ...
  expFrom += pageSize;
  // Remove or increase this limit if you expect more than 1000 results
  if (expFrom >= 5000) keepGoingExps = false;
}
```

---

## **References**
- [Pagination](/developer-docs/apis/automation-api-rest/get-started/get-started#pagination)
- [Archived experiments](/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/manage-your-web-experiments/manage-your-experiments#archived-experiments)

---

If you try these adjustments and still only get 5 results, please provide the raw API response for further troubleshooting.

traces.response:
Your script is already very well-structured and commented. However, you requested a "correction" but did not specify a particular error or issue. Below are some general best-practice corrections and suggestions for improvement, based on common pitfalls and Kameleoon API usage:

---

### 1. **Error Handling for API Calls**
You are using `muteHttpExceptions: true` in your API calls, but you do not always check for non-200 responses. It's best to always check the response code and handle errors accordingly.

**Example Correction:**
```javascript
const response = UrlFetchApp.fetch(url, { ... });
if (response.getResponseCode() !== 200) {
  Logger.log(`API Error: ${response.getResponseCode()} - ${response.getContentText()}`);
  return; // or handle error
}
```

---

### 2. **Token Expiry Handling**
If your script runs for a long time, the OAuth token may expire. Consider caching the token and refreshing it only when needed.

---

### 3. **API Rate Limiting**
Kameleoon APIs may have rate limits. You already use `Utilities.sleep()`, which is good. If you encounter 429 errors, you may want to increase the sleep duration.

---

### 4. **Use of Deprecated Fields**
Some fields like `dateStarted`, `dateEnded`, `startDate`, `stopDate`, etc., may be deprecated in future API versions. Always check the [Kameleoon API reference](https://developers.kameleoon.com/api-reference/overview) for the latest field names.

---

### 5. **Feature Flag Results Endpoint**
You are using `/feature-flags/{id}/results` for feature flag results. This is correct as per the [API documentation](https://developers.kameleoon.com/api-reference/feature-flag-results).

---

### 6. **General Code Cleanliness**
- Consider using `const` and `let` instead of `var` for variable declarations.
- Use strict equality (`===`) everywhere.
- Always check for null/undefined before accessing object properties.

---

### 7. **Script Properties Validation**
Before making API calls, ensure all required script properties (`KAMELEOON_CLIENT_ID`, `KAMELEOON_CLIENT_SECRET`, `KAMELEOON_SITE_ID`) are set, and provide a clear error message if not.

---

### 8. **Documentation Reference**
For more details on the endpoints and payloads, refer to:
- [API Reference Overview](/api-reference/overview)
- [Experiments API](/api-reference/experiments)
- [Feature Flags API](/api-reference/feature-flags)
- [Results API](/api-reference/results)

---

### 9. **Sample Correction for Token Fetch**
```javascript
function getKameleoonAccessToken_() {
  const props = PropertiesService.getScriptProperties();
  const clientId = props.getProperty("KAMELEOON_CLIENT_ID");
  const clientSecret = props.getProperty("KAMELEOON_CLIENT_SECRET");
  if (!clientId || !clientSecret) {
    throw new Error("KAMELEOON_CLIENT_ID or KAMELEOON_CLIENT_SECRET not set in script properties.");
  }
  const response = UrlFetchApp.fetch(KAMELEOON_TOKEN_URL, {
    method: "post",
    contentType: "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
    payload: {
      grant_type: "client_credentials",
      client_id: clientId,
      client_secret: clientSecret
    }
  });
  if (response.getResponseCode() !== 200) {
    throw new Error("Failed to fetch Kameleoon access token: " + response.getContentText());
  }
  return JSON.parse(response.getContentText()).access_token;
}
```

---

### 10. **API Versioning**
You use `v=2.0` in the experiments endpoint. Make sure this matches the latest supported version in the [API documentation](/api-reference/experiments).

---

**If you have a specific error message or issue, please provide it for a more targeted correction.**  
Otherwise, your script is robust and aligns well with Kameleoon's API structure as of the latest documentation.

---

**References:**
- [API Reference Overview](/api-reference/overview)
- [Experiments API](/api-reference/experiments)
- [Feature Flags API](/api-reference/feature-flags)
- [Results API](/api-reference/results)

Based on your script and your issue ("I am still only getting 5 tests"), the likely cause is a limitation in the API request or pagination logic.

From the Kameleoon documentation:

- The API returns up to 200 active experiments per request. To retrieve additional pages, you must increment the page parameter in subsequent requests. See: /developer-docs/apis/automation-api-rest/tutorials/paramsio-use-cases#step-3-handle-the-response
- If you are using the /experiments or /feature-flags endpoints, you need to use the size and from (or page) parameters to paginate through all available experiments or feature flags.

In your script, for both experiments and feature flags, you are using:
```js
let url = `https://api.kameleoon.com/experiments?v=2.0&size=${pageSize}&from=${expFrom}`;
```
and
```js
let url = `https://api.kameleoon.com/feature-flags?size=${pageSize}&from=${featFrom}`;
```
with pageSize = 100 and incrementing expFrom/featFrom by 100 each time. This is correct for pagination.

However, if you are still only getting 5 tests, possible reasons are:
1. The API is only returning 5 results because of a filter (e.g., siteId, status, or other query parameters).
2. Your site/account may only have 5 archived/stopped experiments or feature flags matching your criteria.
3. There may be an issue with authentication or permissions, limiting the results.

**What to check:**
- Ensure your siteId is correct and that there are more than 5 archived/stopped experiments or feature flags in your Kameleoon account.
- Check the API response directly (log the raw response) to see if the API is returning only 5 results, or if your script is filtering them down to 5.
- Make sure you are not hitting any undocumented API limits or errors (check for HTTP 429 or 403 responses).

**Documentation references:**
- Pagination and limits: /developer-docs/apis/automation-api-rest/tutorials/paramsio-use-cases#step-3-handle-the-response
- Export experiments: /api-reference/experiment/export-experiments

If you need to retrieve more than 5, ensure your pagination logic is working and that your account actually has more than 5 matching records. If the API is returning only 5 per page, try increasing the size parameter (up to 200).

If you need further help, please provide the raw API response or any error messages you are seeing.

traces.prompt:
Ich möchte zwei Kampagnen voneinander ausschließen. Reicht es, wenn ich den Ausschluss im Targeting von Kampagne A definiere? 

Current Date: 19/06/2026 11:49
traces.response:
Nein, es reicht nicht, den Ausschluss nur im Targeting von Kampagne A zu definieren, wenn du sicherstellen möchtest, dass sich zwei Kampagnen gegenseitig ausschließen. 

Der empfohlene Weg in Kameleoon ist, sogenannte "Mutually Exclusive Groups" (MEG) zu verwenden. Damit stellst du sicher, dass jeder Besucher nur einer der Kampagnen innerhalb dieser Gruppe zugeordnet wird – und zwar automatisch und konsistent. Dazu musst du beide Kampagnen mit demselben Tag nach dem Muster ME-GROUP-{GROUP NAME} versehen (z. B. ME-GROUP-A). Erst dann sorgt Kameleoon dafür, dass ein Besucher nur eine der Kampagnen sieht.

Wenn du den Ausschluss nur im Targeting von Kampagne A definierst, kann es passieren, dass Besucher trotzdem in Kampagne B landen, obwohl sie schon in A waren (oder umgekehrt). Für echten gegenseitigen Ausschluss muss die Logik für beide Kampagnen gelten.

Weitere Details findest du in der offiziellen Dokumentation:
- Wie funktionieren Mutually Exclusive Groups? → /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-features/prevent-overlapping-campaigns-with-mutually-exclusive-groups
- Vorgehen für Feature-Experimente: /user-manual/experimentation/feature-experimentation/create-and-manage-flags/mutually-exclusive-groups-for-feature-experiments

Kurzum: Nutze Mutually Exclusive Groups und tagge beide Kampagnen entsprechend, um einen echten gegenseitigen Ausschluss zu gewährleisten.

in a second paragraph starting with "Recommendations:".

Leverage your statistical knowledge to analyse each piece of information
before crafting your final output.

If the experiment seems valid — meaning that the setup and data collection worked as intended
(e.g., traffic flowed, metrics were measured, no issues spotted) — then the recommendation can be:
  1. Ship for success if a key metric improves without regressions;
  2. Abort and iterate if regressions are detected and metrics worsen;
  3. Iterate, abandon or ship if it's neutral but informative — when no effect is detected

traces.prompt:
If I want to show the test to people coming in only from specific acquisition channels should i do segment: all visitors and triggers as my acquisition channels? 

Current Date: 19/06/2026 10:38
traces.response:
To target your experiment (test) to visitors coming from specific acquisition channels in Kameleoon, you should use segments—not triggers—for this purpose.

Here’s how you should set it up:

- **Segment:** Create a segment that includes only visitors from your desired acquisition channels. Acquisition channels are a segmentation condition in Kameleoon, allowing you to target visitors based on their traffic source (e.g., paid search, email, social, etc.).
- **Trigger:** Triggers are typically used to launch an experiment based on user actions or events (like clicking a button or scrolling), not for acquisition channel targeting.

**Steps:**
1. Create custom acquisition channels if you haven’t already. See: [Create an acquisition channel](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/advanced-targeting-tools/create-an-acquisition-channel)
2. When defining your experiment’s targeting, create a segment with a condition like “Acquisition channel is [your desired channel(s)].”
3. Assign this segment to your experiment.

**Reference:**  
- [Create an acquisition channel](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/advanced-targeting-tools/create-an-acquisition-channel)
- [Manage advanced targeting tools](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/advanced-targeting-tools/manage-advanced-targeting-tools)

**Summary:**  
- Do NOT use “all visitors” as your segment if you want to restrict by acquisition channel.
- Do NOT use triggers for acquisition channel targeting.
- Use a segment with acquisition channel conditions.

If you need step-by-step instructions on creating such a segment, let me know!

traces.prompt:
Okay, if I set up acquisition channels, is setting up the trigger enough or do I need to set them up somewhere else as well? 

Current Date: 19/06/2026 10:35
traces.response:
To use acquisition channels in your experiments, you need to do two things:

1. **Create and configure the acquisition channels** in the Kameleoon app under Advanced Targeting Tools. This is where you define what constitutes each acquisition channel (e.g., by referrer URL, current page URL, or custom JavaScript).
   - Reference: [Create a new acquisition channel](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/advanced-targeting-tools/create-an-acquisition-channel#create-a-new-acquisition-channel)

2. **Set up a trigger in your experiment** that uses the acquisition channel as a condition. When you add a trigger to your experiment, you can select "Acquisition channel" as the condition to include or exclude visitors based on their acquisition channel.
   - Reference: [Set up a trigger in an experiment](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/triggers/set-up-a-trigger-in-a-campaign#set-up-a-trigger-in-an-experiment)
   - Reference: [Acquisition trigger](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/triggers/create-a-trigger#acquisition)

You do not need to set up acquisition channels anywhere else. Once they are created and configured, simply using them as a trigger in your experiment is enough for targeting.

In summary: 
- Define your acquisition channels in the Advanced Targeting Tools section.
- Use them as a trigger in your experiment’s targeting.

No additional setup is required beyond these steps.

traces.prompt:
is there a way to simulate acquisition channel triggers? 

Current Date: 19/06/2026 10:34
traces.response:
Yes, you can simulate acquisition channel triggers in Kameleoon, but the process depends on your use case and the tools you are using:

1. **Simulation via Campaign Simulator**:
   - When simulating a campaign in Kameleoon, the Triggers tab displays the conditions (including acquisition channel triggers) that must be met for your campaign to launch. This allows you to confirm whether each trigger is correctly recognized during simulation.
   - Reference: [Simulate a campaign - Triggers](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/simulate-a-campaign#triggers)

2. **Simulating Acquisition Channel via SDK/API**:
   - If you are using the Kameleoon SDKs (for example, the Elixir SDK), you can simulate page views with specific referrer indices, which are linked to acquisition channels. The referrer index is available in the Kameleoon app on the acquisition channel configuration page.
   - Example (Elixir SDK):
     ```elixir
     alias Kameleoon.Client
     alias Kameleoon.Data.PageView

     # Simulate a page view with a specific referrer index (e.g., [3])
     :ok = Client.add_data(client, visitor_code, PageView.new!("https://example.com", title: "Homepage", referrers: [3]))
     ```
   - Reference: [Elixir SDK - PageView](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/sdks/web-sdks/elixir-sdk#pageview)

3. **Acquisition Channel Assignment**:
   - Acquisition channels are checked after each page view and stored for the visit's duration. You can influence the acquisition channel by simulating visits with different referrers or URL parameters as defined in your acquisition channel configuration.
   - Reference: [Additional information about acquisition channels](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/advanced-targeting-tools/create-an-acquisition-channel#additional-information-about-acquisition-channels)

If you need to test a specific acquisition channel trigger, ensure your simulation (either via the UI or SDK) matches the acquisition channel's definition (e.g., correct referrer, URL parameter, or traffic source).

Let me know if you need a step-by-step guide for a specific SDK or simulation scenario!

Current Date: 19/06/2026 10:21
traces.response:
Yes, the Kameleoon MCP server is able to retrieve the variation code from an experiment. According to the official documentation, the MCP server automates the workflow of:

1. Retrieving experiment results and identifying the winning variation.
2. Extracting the variation's raw code from Kameleoon.

This means the MCP server can access and retrieve the code associated with a specific variation from an experiment, which can then be used for further automation, such as converting it into production-ready code or wrapping it behind a feature flag.

For more details, see:
- The primary workflow: Winning experiment to production: https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/developer-tools/mcp-server#the-primary-workflow-winning-experiment-to-production

If you need step-by-step instructions or API details, let me know!

traces.prompt:
a quoi correspond "inclure tous les paramètres" dans le trigger "exact"

Current Date: 19/06/2026 10:09
traces.response:
La documentation Kameleoon ne fournit pas d'explication directe sur l'option "inclure tous les paramètres" dans le contexte du trigger "exact". Après recherche, ce terme n'apparaît pas dans les pages officielles concernant la création ou la gestion des triggers.

Si vous faites référence à une option visible dans l'interface Kameleoon lors de la configuration d'un trigger de type "exact", il est probable que cette option permette de prendre en compte tous les paramètres de l'URL (query string) lors de l'évaluation du déclencheur. Cela signifie que le trigger ne s'activera que si l'URL correspond exactement, y compris tous les paramètres présents dans la requête.

Cependant, cette interprétation n'est pas explicitement confirmée dans la documentation officielle. Pour une confirmation ou un détail précis, il est recommandé de contacter le support Kameleoon ou de consulter l'aide contextuelle dans l'interface.

Pour plus d'informations sur la création et la gestion des triggers, vous pouvez consulter la documentation officielle :
- Création d'un trigger : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/triggers/create-a-trigger
- Définir le ciblage d'une expérience : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/define-your-experiments-targeting

N'hésitez pas à préciser le contexte ou à fournir une capture d'écran pour une aide plus ciblée.

traces.response:
Your script logic for pulling archived experiments is mostly correct, but there are a few things to clarify and potentially improve based on the Kameleoon API documentation:

### 1. How to Identify Archived Experiments

- The correct way to identify archived experiments is by checking the `isArchived` property on each experiment object. This is confirmed in the API documentation for the "Get an experiment" endpoint:  
  > Indicates if experiment is archived  
  ([/api-reference/experiment/get-an-experiment](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/experiment/get-an-experiment))

- Your script already checks for `test.isArchived === true` in both legacy and modern streams, which is correct.

### 2. Filtering Only Archived Experiments

- Your current filter includes experiments with status `"stopped"` or `"paused"` or `isArchived === true`.  
  If you want only archived experiments, you should filter **only** where `isArchived === true`.  
  If you want to include stopped/paused but not archived, you would use the status.  
  If you want only archived, change:
  ```js
  if (status === "stopped" || status === "paused" || test.isArchived === true)
  ```
  to:
  ```js
  if (test.isArchived === true)
  ```

### 3. API Pagination

- The API endpoints you use (`/experiments` and `/experiments?v=2.0`) support pagination with the `size` parameter. If you have more than 500 experiments, you need to implement pagination by using the `from` or `page` parameter to fetch all results.  
  See: [/api-reference/experiment/get-all-experiments](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/experiment/get-all-experiments)

### 4. API Response Structure

- The script assumes the response is either an array or has `.experiments` or `.data`. This is generally correct, but always check the actual API response for your account.

### 5. Feature Flags

- If you want to include archived Feature Flags, you should use the `/feature-flags` endpoint and filter by the `archived` property.  
  See: [/api-reference/featureflag/get-all-feature-flags](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/featureflag/get-all-feature-flags)

---

## Summary of Fixes

- **Filter only on `isArchived === true`** for archived experiments.
- **Implement pagination** if you have more than 500 experiments.
- **(Optional)** If you want to include Feature Flags, use the `/feature-flags` endpoint and filter by `archived: true`.

---

## Example Fix for Filtering

Replace:
```js
if (status === "stopped" || status === "paused" || test.isArchived === true) {
```
with:
```js
if (test.isArchived === true) {
```

---

## References

- [Get all experiments](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/experiment/get-all-experiments)
- [Get an experiment](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/experiment/get-an-experiment)
- [Get all feature flags](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/featureflag/get-all-feature-flags)

If you need help with pagination or want to include feature flags, let me know!

traces.prompt:
si je lance un test avec une variante A poussée à 100%, puis que je pause la variante A et que je lance une variante B à 100%: si je n'applique pas la réallocation de trafi avancée, est-ce que les returning visitors vont encore voir la variante A? est-ce conseillé de faire ce type de tests?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 09:56
traces.response:
Voici la réponse détaillée basée sur la documentation officielle Kameleoon :

### 1. Que se passe-t-il pour les returning visitors si on ne fait pas de réallocation avancée ?

Si vous lancez un test avec la variante A à 100%, puis que vous mettez A en pause et lancez la variante B à 100% **sans utiliser la réallocation avancée**, les visiteurs qui avaient déjà été exposés à la variante A continueront de voir la variante A lors de leurs prochaines visites (tant que leur identifiant visiteur/cookie n’a pas expiré). Les nouveaux visiteurs, eux, verront la variante B.

Cela s’explique par le fonctionnement du “bucketing” Kameleoon : l’attribution à une variation est déterministe pour chaque visiteur (via un identifiant stocké côté client). Tant que la réallocation avancée n’est pas appliquée, le système conserve l’attribution initiale pour chaque visiteur, même si la répartition du trafic change.

Référence :  
- /developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#if-i-change-the-traffic-distribution/exposition-will-visitors-still-see-their-allocated-variation

### 2. Que fait la réallocation avancée ?

La réallocation avancée (“Advanced reallocation”) permet de “remettre à zéro” l’attribution des variations pour tous les visiteurs. Après application, tous les visiteurs (anciens et nouveaux) sont re-bucketés selon la nouvelle répartition du trafic. Les returning visitors peuvent donc voir une nouvelle variation lors de leur retour.

Référence :  
- /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/finalizing-an-experiment#advanced-reallocation

### 3. Est-ce conseillé de faire ce type de test (A 100% puis B 100% sans réallocation) ?

Ce n’est **pas conseillé** si votre objectif est d’obtenir des résultats fiables et exploitables. En effet :
- Vous créez deux populations distinctes : les anciens visiteurs restent sur A, les nouveaux sur B.
- Cela fausse l’analyse, car le groupe “B” ne sera composé que de nouveaux visiteurs, tandis que “A” contiendra uniquement des returning visitors.
- Vous ne pouvez pas comparer proprement les performances des deux variations, car les populations ne sont pas équivalentes.

La bonne pratique est :
- Soit d’utiliser la réallocation avancée pour “remettre à zéro” l’attribution et garantir que tous les visiteurs suivent la nouvelle règle de répartition.
- Soit de dupliquer le test et de lancer un nouveau test pour la variante B, afin d’avoir des populations propres et des résultats interprétables.

---

**Résumé :**
- Sans réallocation avancée, les returning visitors voient toujours leur ancienne variation.
- Ce n’est pas recommandé pour des tests A/B fiables.
- Utilisez la réallocation avancée ou dupliquez le test pour garantir la validité statistique.

Pour plus de détails :
- /developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#if-i-change-the-traffic-distribution/exposition-will-visitors-still-see-their-allocated-variation
- /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/finalizing-an-experiment#advanced-reallocation

traces.prompt:
quand on change l'allocation de trafic dun test,est-il préférable de dupliquer le test et d'en lancer un nouveau, ou d'utiliser l'option de rallocation? et pourquoi?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 09:52
traces.response:
La documentation Kameleoon ne donne pas de recommandation explicite sur le choix entre dupliquer un test ou utiliser la réallocation du trafic, mais voici les bonnes pratiques et points clés à considérer selon la documentation officielle :

- Modifier l’allocation du trafic d’un test en cours (réallocation) est possible directement dans l’interface Kameleoon. Cela permet d’ajuster la répartition du trafic entre les variations sans arrêter ni dupliquer le test. Voir : /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/setting-up-multivariate-tests#traffic-distribution-by-sections

- Cependant, changer l’allocation du trafic en cours de test peut avoir un impact sur la validité statistique des résultats, notamment en introduisant un risque de Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM), c’est-à-dire un écart entre la répartition attendue et la répartition réelle des visiteurs. Kameleoon détecte automatiquement les SRM et alerte si un déséquilibre critique apparaît. Voir : /user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch

- Si vous souhaitez garantir une analyse statistique “propre” et éviter tout biais lié à un changement d’allocation en cours de route, il est préférable de dupliquer le test et de lancer une nouvelle expérience avec la nouvelle allocation. Cela permet de séparer les populations et d’obtenir des résultats plus fiables pour chaque configuration de trafic.

- Si l’objectif est purement opérationnel (par exemple, augmenter rapidement l’exposition d’une variation gagnante), la réallocation peut être utilisée, mais il faut surveiller les alertes SRM et interpréter les résultats avec prudence.

En résumé :
- Pour une analyse statistique rigoureuse : dupliquez le test et repartez à zéro.
- Pour un ajustement opérationnel rapide : utilisez la réallocation, mais surveillez les SRM.

Références :
- Réallocation du trafic : /user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/setting-up-multivariate-tests#traffic-distribution-by-sections
- Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM) : /user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch

N’hésitez pas à préciser votre contexte si vous souhaitez une recommandation adaptée à votre cas d’usage !

traces.prompt:
one question i have about this when it comes to how monthly active users are calculated.

so if i have a player on mweb and then they go to desktop and then native app.

with the kameleooncrossdeviceuuid set up, these users would be counted as one user towards our monthly limit?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 09:34
traces.response:
Yes, if you have set up cross-device history reconciliation using a unique identifier (such as kameleoonCrossDeviceUUID or a custom data value), Kameleoon will count a user who visits from multiple devices (e.g., mobile web, desktop, and native app) as a single unique user towards your monthly limit.

Kameleoon merges sessions across devices based on the unique identifier you provide. This means that as long as the same identifier is used for the user on each device, all their sessions will be grouped and counted as one unique user for monthly active user (MAU) or monthly tracked user (MTU) calculations.

From the documentation:
> "Kameleoon counts users only once, even if they participate in different experiments or feature flags across multiple devices."  
Source: [/user-manual/account-and-team-management/organization-settings/how-kameleoon-tracks-and-counts-unique-users](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/account-and-team-management/organization-settings/how-kameleoon-tracks-and-counts-unique-users)

And:
> "When a user visits a website multiple times via different devices, Kameleoon identifies them as a returning visitor and accurately reports the number of visits... This approach ensures that unique visitor metrics remain accurate, even if the user is not yet identified in the customer system."  
Source: [/developer-docs/cross-device-experimentation](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/cross-device-experimentation)

So, with cross-device reconciliation properly set up, your scenario (player on mweb, then desktop, then native app) will be counted as one user, not three.

For more details, see:
- [How Kameleoon tracks and counts unique users](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/account-and-team-management/organization-settings/how-kameleoon-tracks-and-counts-unique-users)
- [Cross-device history reconciliation](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/cross-device-experimentation)

traces.prompt:
Kann ich ein Bundle mit eingebetten Instagram Reels erstellen?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 09:31
traces.response:
In der offiziellen Kameleoon-Dokumentation gibt es keine direkte Anleitung oder Erwähnung, wie man ein Bundle mit eingebetteten Instagram Reels erstellt. Die Dokumentation beschreibt, wie man Widgets mit dem Widget Studio erstellt und wie man verschiedene Elemente (wie z.B. Text, Links, Wheel of Fortune) einfügt, aber es gibt keinen spezifischen Abschnitt zum Einbetten von Instagram Reels.

Allgemein gilt:
- Du kannst im Widget Studio eigene HTML-Elemente und benutzerdefinierten Code einfügen. Wenn Instagram einen offiziellen Embed-Code für Reels bereitstellt, könntest du diesen theoretisch in ein benutzerdefiniertes Widget einbauen.
- Die Dokumentation beschreibt, wie du Widgets „Fixed in-page“ platzierst und wie du Templates verwaltest, aber nicht explizit das Einbetten von externen Social-Media-Inhalten wie Instagram Reels.

Weitere Informationen zu Widgets findest du hier:
- Widgets erstellen: /user-manual/assets/widgets/create-a-widget-with-the-widget-studio/create-a-widget
- Widgets im Seiteninhalt platzieren: /user-manual/assets/widgets/create-a-widget-with-the-widget-studio/simulate-your-widgets

Fazit: Ein direktes Feature für Instagram Reels gibt es nicht, aber über benutzerdefinierten HTML-Code im Widget könnte es technisch möglich sein. Eine offizielle Anleitung dazu existiert jedoch nicht in der Kameleoon-Dokumentation.

traces.prompt:
différence tous les visiteurs et visiteurs ciblés par une campagne

Current Date: 19/06/2026 08:56
traces.response:
Voici la différence entre "tous les visiteurs" et "visiteurs ciblés" par une campagne dans Kameleoon :

- Tous les visiteurs : Cela signifie que la campagne cible l’ensemble du trafic, sans aucune restriction ni critère de ciblage. Tous les utilisateurs qui visitent votre site peuvent être inclus dans la campagne ou l’expérimentation.
  - Référence : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/define-your-experiments-targeting#all-visitors

- Visiteurs ciblés : Ce sont uniquement les visiteurs qui remplissent les critères de ciblage définis pour la campagne (par exemple, une audience spécifique, des conditions de navigation, des segments comportementaux, etc.). Seuls ces visiteurs verront la campagne ou l’expérimentation.
  - Référence : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/get-started/using-the-homepage#targeted-unique-visitors

En résumé :
- "Tous les visiteurs" = aucune restriction, tout le trafic est inclus.
- "Visiteurs ciblés" = seuls les visiteurs correspondant à des critères précis sont inclus.

N’hésitez pas si vous souhaitez des exemples de critères de ciblage ou plus de détails sur la configuration !
