traces.prompt:
possiamo trarre qualche conclusione da questo test?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 08:27

User is currently looking at the EXPERIMENT with ID: 394336
traces.response:
Per capire se puoi trarre delle conclusioni dal test (esperimento ID: 394336) su Kameleoon, devi verificare alcuni aspetti chiave:

### 1. Stato dei risultati dell’esperimento
Kameleoon assegna a ogni esperimento uno di questi tre stati, in base all’affidabilità statistica raggiunta:
- **Win**: La variazione ha superato il controllo e ha raggiunto la soglia di affidabilità.
- **Loss**: La variazione ha performato peggio del controllo e ha raggiunto la soglia di affidabilità.
- **Inconclusive**: I risultati non hanno raggiunto la soglia di affidabilità richiesta. Non c’è abbastanza evidenza statistica per confermare un impatto positivo o negativo della variazione.

Se il tuo esperimento è “Win” o “Loss”, puoi trarre una conclusione affidabile. Se è “Inconclusive”, non puoi trarre conclusioni definitive e potrebbe essere necessario prolungare il test o aumentare il traffico.

> Riferimento: [user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/takeaways-menu#experiment-results](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/takeaways-menu#experiment-results)

### 2. Significatività statistica
Kameleoon calcola automaticamente la significatività statistica (p-value e affidabilità) in base agli obiettivi impostati. Un risultato è considerato statisticamente significativo se la probabilità di errore (p-value) è inferiore alla soglia scelta (tipicamente 0.05, cioè 95% di affidabilità).

> Riferimento: [user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/statistical-significance](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/statistical-significance)

### 3. Durata e traffico
Un test deve essere eseguito abbastanza a lungo e con sufficiente traffico per raggiungere la significatività. Se il test è stato troppo breve o ha avuto poco traffico, i risultati potrebbero essere inconcludenti.

> Riferimento: [user-manual/faq#experiment-setup-and-methodology](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/faq#experiment-setup-and-methodology)

### 4. SRM (Sample Ratio Mismatch)
Assicurati che non ci siano problemi di SRM (distribuzione non corretta del traffico tra le varianti), che possono invalidare i risultati.

> Riferimento: [user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch#why-is-srm-relevant](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch#why-is-srm-relevant)

---

**In sintesi:**  
- Se il test è “Win” o “Loss” e la significatività statistica è stata raggiunta, puoi trarre una conclusione affidabile.
- Se è “Inconclusive”, non puoi trarre conclusioni definitive.
- Verifica sempre che non ci siano problemi di traffico o SRM.

Per vedere lo stato preciso del tuo esperimento, consulta la pagina dei risultati su Kameleoon:  
[user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/access-the-results-page](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/access-the-results-page)

Se vuoi dettagli su come interpretare i risultati, consulta anche:  
[user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/takeaways-menu#experiment-results](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/takeaways-menu#experiment-results)

traces.prompt:
in GA4 haben wir aktuell quasi keine Daten zu dem Test 47. Die Verknüpfung im Test ist da.

Hast du eine Idee warum hier so wenig ankommt (3 Wochen läuft er schon)?

Andere Tests sehen soweit Okay aus.

 

Kann es daran liegen, dass der Test 2x angelegt ist? Aktuell noch als Entwurf und einmal als Code?

Es ist auch seltsam das 2 verschiedene Namen übergeben werden.

Current Date: 19/06/2026 08:27
traces.response:
Hier sind einige mögliche Ursachen, warum in GA4 für Test 47 kaum Daten ankommen, obwohl die Verknüpfung im Test vorhanden ist und andere Tests korrekt Daten liefern:

1. Test doppelt angelegt (Entwurf & Code):
   - Wenn ein Test zweimal existiert (z.B. einmal als Entwurf und einmal als aktiver Test mit Code), kann es zu Verwirrung kommen, welcher Test tatsächlich ausgespielt und getrackt wird. Nur der veröffentlichte und aktiv laufende Test sendet Daten an GA4. Ein Entwurf sendet keine Daten.
   - Prüfe, ob beide Tests eventuell unterschiedliche Namen oder IDs haben. Das kann dazu führen, dass in GA4 zwei verschiedene Events/Namen erscheinen und die Daten sich aufsplitten.

2. Unterschiedliche Namen in der Übertragung:
   - Wenn zwei verschiedene Namen an GA4 übergeben werden, kann das bedeuten, dass entweder unterschiedliche Tests oder unterschiedliche Varianten getrackt werden. Prüfe in Kameleoon, wie der Testname und die Variantennamen konfiguriert sind und ob sie mit den in GA4 ankommenden Events übereinstimmen.

3. GA4 Identifier:
   - Stelle sicher, dass der richtige GA4 Identifier verwendet wird (z.B. `ga4_client_id` für Webprojekte). Falsche oder fehlende Identifier können dazu führen, dass keine oder zu wenige Daten ankommen. Siehe: https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/integrations/as-a-destination/analytics/google-analytics-4/google-analytics-4-event-streaming#choosing-the-right-ga4-identifier

4. Integration korrekt aktiviert?
   - Die Integration muss im Kameleoon-Projekt aktiviert und korrekt konfiguriert sein. Nach Aktivierung kann es bis zu 24 Stunden dauern, bis Daten in GA4 sichtbar sind. Siehe: https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/integrations/as-a-destination/analytics/google-analytics-4/google-analytics-4-explorations#step-1-enable-the-integration-in-kameleoon

5. Event-Konfiguration:
   - Prüfe, ob die Events für den Test korrekt an GA4 gesendet werden. Bei manchen Zielen (Goals) muss in GA4 ein entsprechendes Event/Audience manuell angelegt werden, damit die Daten sichtbar werden. Siehe: https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#send-to-external-tools

Empfehlung:
- Lösche oder archiviere den Entwurf, sodass nur der aktive Test existiert.
- Vergleiche die Namen/IDs in Kameleoon und GA4.
- Prüfe die Event-Konfiguration und den verwendeten Identifier.
- Kontrolliere, ob im Kameleoon-Dashboard für Test 47 Besucher und Conversions korrekt gezählt werden. Wenn ja, liegt das Problem wahrscheinlich an der Übertragung zu GA4.

Weitere Details findest du in der offiziellen Dokumentation:
- GA4 Integration: /user-manual/integrations/as-a-destination/analytics/google-analytics-4/setting-up-google-analytics-4
- GA4 Identifier: /user-manual/integrations/as-a-destination/analytics/google-analytics-4/google-analytics-4-event-streaming
- Events an externe Tools: /user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal

Wenn du konkrete Eventnamen oder IDs aus GA4 und Kameleoon vergleichst, kann das helfen, die Ursache weiter einzugrenzen.

Focus only on the improvement or decline of metrics and the reliability of their estimates.

Provide clear, actionable recommendations based on the trends observed
in a second paragraph starting with "Recommendations:".

Leverage your statistical knowledge to analyse each piece of information
before crafting your final output.

If the experiment seems valid — meaning that the setup and data collection worked as intended
(e.g., traffic flowed, metrics were measured, no issues spotted) — then the recommendation can be:
  1. Ship for success if a key metric improves without regressions;
  2. Abort and iterate if regressions are detected and metrics worsen;
  3. Iterate, abandon or ship if it's neutral but informative — when no effect is detected
     but the test was strong enough to detect one if it existed.

traces.prompt:
where do i find the global script

Current Date: 19/06/2026 07:45
traces.response:
You can find and edit the Kameleoon global script in your project settings. Here’s how to access it:

1. Go to the Kameleoon platform.
2. Navigate to: Admin > Settings > Configuration > General.
3. Scroll down to find the "Global script" section.

- The global script is a JavaScript snippet injected at the global level of your site/project. It runs on every page where the Kameleoon tag is loaded, before all experiments, personalizations, segments, and triggers.
- Use it to define variables, helper functions, tracking code, or any logic that should be available across all experiments in the project.

For more details and best practices, see the documentation:
- https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/project-management/manage-your-projects#general

If you are using the Kameleoon VS Code extension, the global script is located under the /projects/*/global folder:
- https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/developer-tools/vs-code-extension#global-script

Let me know if you need instructions for a specific environment or integration!

traces.prompt:
/create-goal track all users for Applight site

Current Date: 19/06/2026 07:34
traces.response:
To create a goal that tracks all users for the Applight site in Kameleoon, you typically want to use the default "Engagement" goal or set up a custom goal that triggers for every visitor.

Here’s how you can do it:

1. Go to your Applight project in the Kameleoon App.
2. Navigate to the Goals section.
3. Click “Create a new goal.”
4. For tracking all users, you can:
   - Use the default **Engagement** goal (tracks all users who visit more than one page).
   - Or, create a **Custom goal** and set it to trigger for every user (for example, on page load).

For a custom goal that tracks all users:
- Select **Custom goal** as the type.
- Name your goal (e.g., “Track All Users”).
- In the trigger setup, configure it to fire on every page view or visit.

For more details, see the official guide:  
https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal

If you want to automate this via API or need a code snippet to trigger the goal for every user, let me know!

traces.prompt:
article sur le changement d'allocation de trafic dans un test et sur le fait d'arrêter une variante en cours de route

Current Date: 19/06/2026 07:27
traces.response:
Voici les ressources officielles Kameleoon concernant le changement d’allocation de trafic dans un test et l’arrêt d’une variante en cours de route :

### 1. Changement d’allocation de trafic dans un test

- **Méthodes d’allocation du trafic**  
  Vous pouvez choisir entre une allocation manuelle (statique), Multi-armed Bandit (automatique selon la performance des variantes), ou Contextual Bandit (personnalisation dynamique selon les attributs visiteurs).  
  Voir la documentation :  
  [user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/finalizing-an-experiment#allocation-method](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/configure-and-launch/finalizing-an-experiment#allocation-method)

- **Gestion de l’allocation du trafic**  
  Pour ajuster la répartition du trafic, il suffit de déplacer le curseur dans l’interface Kameleoon pour exclure un pourcentage de visiteurs ou modifier la part attribuée à chaque variante.  
  Voir la documentation :  
  [user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/setting-up-multivariate-tests#traffic-allocation-for-the-experiment](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experimentation/web-experimentation/advanced-experiment-types/setting-up-multivariate-tests#traffic-allocation-for-the-experiment)

- **FAQ sur la modification de la répartition du trafic**  
  Si vous modifiez la distribution du trafic en cours de test, Kameleoon garantit que les visiteurs déjà exposés à une variante continueront de voir la même, sauf si vous forcez une nouvelle attribution.  
  Voir la FAQ :  
  [developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#why-is-the-visitor-allocation-in-my-reports-incorrect](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#why-is-the-visitor-allocation-in-my-reports-incorrect)

### 2. Arrêter une variante en cours de route

- **Désactivation d’une variante**  
  Vous pouvez désactiver une variante à tout moment. Les visiteurs qui n’ont pas encore été exposés à cette variante ne la verront plus. Ceux déjà exposés continueront de la voir, sauf si vous réattribuez explicitement les visiteurs.  
  Voir la FAQ (section “If I change the traffic distribution/exposition, will visitors still see their allocated variation?”) :  
  [developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#why-is-the-visitor-allocation-in-my-reports-incorrect](https://docs.kameleoon.com/developer-docs/feature-experimentation/technical-reference/faq-global#why-is-the-visitor-allocation-in-my-reports-incorrect)

### Points importants

- Le changement d’allocation ou l’arrêt d’une variante peut entraîner un Sample Ratio Mismatch (SRM) si la répartition réelle ne correspond plus à la répartition attendue. Kameleoon détecte automatiquement ce type de déséquilibre.  
  [user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch#what-is-sample-ratio-mismatch](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/statistical-methods/sample-ratio-mismatch#what-is-sample-ratio-mismatch)

N’hésitez pas à préciser votre cas d’usage (web, feature flag, SDK, etc.) pour des instructions détaillées adaptées à votre contexte.

traces.prompt:
explique moi en français et avec des mots simples en quoi le fait de stopper une variante dans un A/B/C test alors qu'il est en ligne, et ensuite de réallouer ultérieurement le trafic sur les 2 variantes restantes (original et variante 1) peut biaiser les résultats du test

Current Date: 19/06/2026 07:11
traces.response:
Voici une explication simple en français :

Lorsque tu fais un test A/B/C (donc avec 3 variantes : l’originale, variante 1 et variante 2), chaque visiteur est réparti aléatoirement entre ces 3 variantes. Si tu décides d’arrêter une des variantes (par exemple la variante 2) alors que le test est déjà en ligne, puis que tu réalloues le trafic uniquement sur l’originale et la variante 1, cela peut biaiser les résultats pour plusieurs raisons :

1. **Changement de répartition** : Au début, les visiteurs étaient répartis sur 3 variantes. Après l’arrêt de la variante 2, la répartition change. Les nouveaux visiteurs ne sont plus comparables aux anciens, car ils n’ont pas eu la même chance de voir chaque variante.

2. **Effet de temporalité** : Les visiteurs qui ont vu la variante 2 l’ont vue à un moment précis (par exemple, en début de campagne). Si le comportement des visiteurs change dans le temps (saisonnalité, promotions, etc.), les résultats entre les périodes ne sont plus comparables.

3. **Population différente** : Les visiteurs qui arrivent après l’arrêt de la variante 2 ne sont pas forcément les mêmes que ceux d’avant. Cela peut fausser la comparaison entre les variantes restantes.

4. **Interprétation difficile** : Les statistiques du test deviennent moins fiables, car tu compares des groupes qui n’ont pas été exposés aux mêmes conditions tout au long du test.

En résumé : arrêter une variante en cours de route et redistribuer le trafic fausse l’équilibre du test et rend les résultats moins fiables. Pour garder des résultats valides, il vaut mieux éviter de modifier la répartition des variantes pendant le test.

Pour plus de détails, Kameleoon recommande de ne pas modifier un test en ligne, ou alors de le dupliquer et de relancer un nouveau test pour garantir la fiabilité des résultats. Source : https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/faq#can-i-edit-an-online-experiment

N’hésite pas si tu veux des exemples ou des explications plus techniques !

Current Date: 19/06/2026 06:36
traces.response:
Based on the Kameleoon documentation, here’s how conversion triggers work and why you’re seeing all users who visit /product/ or /collections/ pages being reported, rather than only those defined in your code:

### How Conversion Triggers Work

- **UI-Defined Triggers:** If you set up a conversion trigger in the Kameleoon UI (for example, for all /pages/ or /collections/), this trigger will fire for any user who matches those page conditions, regardless of what is in your custom code.
- **Custom Code Triggers:** If you want to restrict conversion reporting to only specific actions or pages, you should use custom JavaScript code with the `triggerGoal()` method. This allows you to programmatically control exactly when a conversion is reported (for example, only after a specific action on a specific page).

### Why the UI Trigger is Overruling Your Code

If you have both:
- A trigger set in the UI for /pages/ or /collections/
- And a custom trigger in your code (e.g., only on certain product pages)

…the UI trigger will fire for all matching pageviews, causing conversions to be reported for all users who visit those pages, not just those who meet your custom code conditions.

### How to Fix This

- **Remove or narrow the UI trigger:** Only use the UI trigger if you want all users on those pages to be counted as conversions.
- **Use only custom code for precise control:** Set the trigger to “Custom JavaScript” in the goal setup, and use the `triggerGoal()` function in your code to report conversions only when your specific conditions are met.

#### Example (from documentation):

```javascript
// Only trigger conversion when your custom condition is met
if (window.location.pathname === '/product/specific-product') {
  triggerGoal();
}
```

Or, with revenue:

```javascript
triggerGoal(49.99);
```

**Reference:**  
- [Create a goal – Option 3: Using custom JavaScript code](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#option-3-using-custom-javascript-code)

---

**Summary:**  
The UI trigger will always fire for all users matching its condition, regardless of your code. To restrict conversion reporting to only those defined in your code, remove the broad UI trigger and use only the custom JavaScript trigger with `triggerGoal()`.

If you need step-by-step help updating your setup, let me know!

To create a custom goal named "KAI goal test" for the Applight site in Kameleoon, follow these steps:

1. **Go to Goals Management**: In the Kameleoon App, navigate to the section where you manage goals for your project (Applight).

2. **Create a New Goal**:
   - Click on “Add goal” or “Create a new goal.”
   - In the goal creation form, select **Custom goal** as the type.
   - Enter the name: `KAI goal test`.
   - Fill in any additional details as needed (description, status, etc.).

3. **Set Up the Custom Goal**:
   - After selecting "Custom goal," you will see setup options. Complete the configuration according to your tracking needs.
   - Save the goal.

4. **Trigger the Custom Goal (if needed in code)**:
   - To trigger this goal via JavaScript, use the following code in your site (replace with your actual goal ID if needed):

   ```javascript
   Kameleoon.API.Goals.processConversion(<GOAL_ID>);
   ```
   Or, if you use the custom code panel in the Kameleoon App:
   ```javascript
   triggerGoal();
   ```

For more details and screenshots, see:
- How to create a custom goal: https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#custom-goal
- API reference for creating goals: https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/goal/create-a-new-goal

traces.prompt:
I created a click tracking goal, now how can I monitor or get reports of this goal ?

Current Date: 19/06/2026 05:47
traces.response:
To monitor or get reports for your click tracking goal in Kameleoon, follow these steps:

### 1. View Goal Conversions in Analytics
- Go to the **Metrics** menu in your Kameleoon dashboard.
- Select **view metrics per goal**.
- Click **Add breakdown**.
- Select your click tracking goal from the first selector and any relevant metadata from the second selector.
- Click **Apply** to see detailed reports and breakdowns for your goal.

Reference: [View per goal](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/goal-metadata#view-per-goal)

### 2. Check Goal Health (Conversion Tracking)
- Use the **Conversion Tracking Check** in the Health menu to monitor if your goal is actively receiving conversions.
  - **Passed**: Conversions recorded in the last 24 hours.
  - **Warning**: Zero conversions for at least one goal in the last 24 hours.
  - **Failed**: Zero conversions for more than 48-72 hours (possible tracking issue).

Reference: [Conversion Tracking Check](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/experiment-analytics/analyze-results/health-menu#conversion-tracking-check)

### 3. API Access (Advanced)
If you need to programmatically access goal data, you can use the Kameleoon API:
- [Get a goal](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/goal/get-a-goal)
- [Get all goals](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/goal/get-all-goals)
- [Search goals](https://docs.kameleoon.com/api-reference/goal/search-goals)

These endpoints allow you to retrieve goal details, including conversion counts and metadata.

---

For a step-by-step guide on creating and managing click tracking goals, see:  
[Click tracking goal setup](https://docs.kameleoon.com/user-manual/assets/goals/create-a-goal#click-tracking)

If you need more details on a specific report or dashboard, let me know!
